workItem.title: Batch reconstructible audit checkpoints to reduce provider pressure
workItem.body:

## Context

A measured live outbox contained 2343 pending events (about 6.55 MB), including 168 workspace checkpoints. Each turn/diff/updated checkpoint was durable locally but forced an immediate GitHub audit commit, contributing unnecessary tree/ref/commit operations and secondary rate limiting.

## Expected outcome

Symphony persists every redacted workspace checkpoint immediately in its append-only local outbox but confirms reconstructible checkpoints through the normal 50-event or 60-second audit batch, while terminal and irreversible boundaries continue to flush immediately.

## Scope

- Classify workspace checkpoint events as durable but non-critical for remote flush timing
- Retain immediate append-only local persistence and the complete redacted diff
- Keep turn completion, failure and cancellation as immediate flush boundaries
- Add regression coverage for the flush classification
- Document the durability and provider-pressure tradeoff
- Run the complete Symphony quality gate

## Out of scope

- Drop or aggregate workspace checkpoint events
- Delay terminal or irreversible lifecycle evidence
- Change the GitHub audit schema or batch-size contract

## Acceptance criteria

- A workspace checkpoint is written locally immediately and remains auditable
- A checkpoint alone does not force an immediate remote audit commit
- A batch flushes at 50 events or 60 seconds
- Turn completion, failure and cancellation still flush immediately
- The measured improvement and rationale are recorded in the case study
- The complete Symphony gate passes
